description: WireGuard API endpoints and client config generation (Task Group 4 remaining: 4.3 and 4.4)
steps: Create a new file panel/internal/api/wireguard.go with WireGuard peer management handlers. This keeps the already-large api.go from growing further. The file should be in package api and have methods on *Server.

Implement handler wireguardPeers(w,r) for GET/POST /api/wireguard/peers: GET lists all peers from wg_peers table (joined with customers for username). POST creates a new peer - accepts {customer_id, node_id, allowed_ips} and uses wireguard.GenerateKeyPair() and wireguard.GeneratePresharedKey() to generate keys, inserts into wg_peers, and creates a 'wireguard.add_peer' node task.
Implement handler wireguardPeerByID(w,r) for DELETE /api/wireguard/peers/{id}: looks up peer by ID, sets status='revoked', creates 'wireguard.remove_peer' node task with the public key.

Implement handler wireguardPeerConfig(w,r) for GET /api/wireguard/peers/{id}/config: looks up peer, calls GenerateClientConfig() to build a complete .conf file, returns it as text/plain with Content-Disposition attachment.

Implement GenerateClientConfig(peer WgPeer, serverPublicKey, serverEndpoint string) string function that produces a complete WireGuard .conf file with [Interface] (PrivateKey, Address, DNS) and [Peer] (PublicKey=server, PresharedKey, AllowedIPs=0.0.0.0/0, Endpoint) sections. Place this in panel/internal/wireguard/clientconfig.go.

Register routes in api.go Routes(): mux.HandleFunc('/api/wireguard/peers', s.requireAdmin(s.wireguardPeers)) and mux.HandleFunc('/api/wireguard/peers/', s.requireAdmin(s.wireguardPeerByID)) - the wireguardPeerByID handler should check for '/config' action to route to wireguardPeerConfig.
Write unit tests in panel/internal/wireguard/clientconfig_test.go for GenerateClientConfig verifying the output contains correct sections and keys.
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes including new tests
GenerateClientConfig produces valid WireGuard config format
findings: All acceptance criteria met. go build ./... and go test ./... pass cleanly. WireGuard API handlers placed in separate wireguard.go file following existing code conventions. Routes registered in api.go Routes() method. GenerateClientConfig uses a struct-based parameter for clarity. The config download endpoint queries node_vpn_configs extra_json for server_public_key, dns_1, dns_2 and uses node domain/IP with WireGuard port for the endpoint.
